page: Microsoft Azure M32dms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the MDSMediumMemoryv2 family. It is equipped with 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features a massive memory allocation of 875.0 GB, yielding approximately 27.34 GB of RAM per virtual core.

This virtual machine includes 1049 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, allowing for high-performance disk configurations. There are no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance. The hardware profile is heavily weighted toward memory capacity, offering an exceptionally high memory-to-vCPU ratio designed for memory-intensive workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the M32dms_v2 is positioned as a specialized resource where the premium is justified by the dense memory-to-core ratio rather than raw compute scaling. This makes it highly suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise applications, heavy caching layers, and database workloads requiring significant RAM and local storage capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure M32dms_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MDSMediumMemoryv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Powered by an Intel x86_64 processor, it features 32 dedicated vCPUs and a substantial 875.0 GB of RAM, translating to 27.34 GB of memory per core. The instance includes 1049 GB of local storage and supports premium storage configurations, though it lacks integrated GPUs and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio, this server is designed for memory-intensive enterprise applications, large in-memory databases, and heavy caching workloads where memory capacity is the primary constraint. The hardware configuration prioritizes memory density over raw compute scaling, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for workloads that require massive RAM footprints without the overhead of additional CPU cores.
